system.description: Front-line Coalition infantry with broad battlefield training, basic vehicle familiarity, and the standard Dead Boy field loadout.
system.notes: This class uses expanded class skill pools so Coalition soldier picks resolve against the current imported skill catalog.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Begins with Hand to Hand: Expert. The soldier may instead take Hand to Hand: Martial Arts by giving up one other skill, or Hand to Hand: Assassin if evil by giving up two other skills.

gear: CA-1 Heavy Dead Boy Body Armor

system.description: Coalition power armor and robot specialist trained to operate SAMAS suits, heavy military vehicles, and the sensor systems that keep them alive.
system.notes: This class expects at least three related-skill picks from Pilot. Keep that minimum at the table when leveling.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Begins with Hand to Hand: Expert. The pilot may instead take Martial Arts, or Hand to Hand: Assassin if evil, by giving up two other skills.

gear: CA-1 Heavy Dead Boy Body Armor
Conventional military vehicle for routine duty

system.description: Coalition reconnaissance and espionage officer trained for intelligence gathering, covert work, and sensor-driven battlefield command.
system.notes: This class expects at least four related-skill picks from Espionage. Keep that minimum at the table when leveling.

relatedSkills.name: Paramedic
relatedSkills.notes: Counts as a single class selection in this build. Track the source rule manually if you use another table convention.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Begins with Hand to Hand: Expert. The officer may instead take Hand to Hand: Martial Arts by giving up one other skill, or Hand to Hand: Assassin if evil by giving up one other skill.

system.description: Coalition technical specialist trained in communications, engineering, technical support, or weapons systems through a chosen military occupational specialty.
system.notes: Choose one MOS track at character creation and then pick five skills from that track at +15%. The current class framework does not yet enforce a single-track MOS pool automatically, so handle that part manually at the table.

relatedSkills.name: First Aid
relatedSkills.notes: Paramedic counts as two skill selections under the source rules.

relatedSkills.name: Paramedic
relatedSkills.notes: Paramedic counts as two skill selections under the source rules.

secondarySkills.name: First Aid
secondarySkills.notes: Paramedic counts as two skill selections under the source rules.

secondarySkills.name: Paramedic
secondarySkills.notes: Paramedic counts as two skill selections under the source rules.

grantedAbilities.name: Hand to Hand Training
grantedAbilities.notes: Begins with Hand to Hand: Basic. The officer may upgrade to Expert by giving up one other skill, or to Martial Arts or Hand to Hand: Assassin if evil by giving up two other skills.

grantedAbilities.name: Military Occupational Specialty
grantedAbilities.notes: Choose one track: Communications, Engineering, Technician, or Weapons. Then select five skills from that track at +15%.
